title: Intense Charred Malaysian Tempeh Roast
description: A fiery charred tempeh roast infused with Malaysian aromatics, topped with sea salt and a tangy pickle, perfect for a macrobiotic-friendly feast.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200C and line a baking sheet with parchment.
Slice tempeh into 1 cm thick slabs and pat dry with paper towels.
Whisk soy sauce, coconut oil, grated ginger, minced garlic, Malacca pepper flakes, and sea salt into a bowl.
Brush both sides of each tempeh slab with the sauce mixture.
Arrange slabs on sheet and bake for 12 minutes, flipping halfway.
While baking, warm a small skillet over medium heat and toast the remaining sauce until fragrant.
Remove tempeh, drizzle with the toasted sauce, and sprinkle with lime zest.
Plate the tempeh and top with pickled cucumber slices and chopped cilantro.
Serve immediately with a side of steamed rice or whole grain noodles.
Enjoy this charred, molten temper with a romantic Malaysian flair.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
